narrative_md: Patient self-reported medication events. Each MedicationStatement produces one drug_exposure row with drug_type_concept_id defaulting to 44787730 (Patient Self-Reported Medication). This is the only FHIR medication resource with a normative HL7 IG FML mapping. Dynamic type concept adjustment is possible based on basedOn/partOf references (omoponfhir behavior).

fields.notes: Surrogate key. HL7 IG FML leaves this as a commented-out TODO. omoponfhir uses IdMapping.getOMOPfromFHIR().

sources.comment: HL7 IG FML (normative) leaves drug_exposure_id mapping as a commented-out TODO — no implementation provided

references.notes: person_id and drug_exposure_id commented out as TODO

sources.comment: omoponfhir uses a bidirectional IdMapping registry (getOMOPfromFHIR) to assign the surrogate key

references.notes: constructOmop() — IdMapping.getOMOPfromFHIR() at entry

fields.notes: Resolve Patient reference. HL7 IG FML leaves this as a commented-out TODO.
RxNorm / ATC / NDC mapped to OMOP standard concept. HL7 IG FML maps from medication.concept.coding.code. Placeholder: 0 when concept lookup unavailable.
Tertiary fallback chain unique to MedicationStatement. omoponfhir falls back to new Date() instead of dateAsserted.

sources.comment: HL7 IG FML maps from effective[x] (DateTime or Period.start); no tertiary fallback defined

references.notes: drug_exposure_start_date from effective[x]

sources.comment: This project falls back to dateAsserted as a tertiary source when no effective[x] is present

references.notes: Effective date handling (DateTime vs Period); falls back to new Date() rather than dateAsserted

fields.notes: Full ISO datetime.

fields.transform: effectivePeriod.end; fallback to start_date when effectiveDateTime is used (point in time) or period.end is absent
fields.notes: When effectiveDateTime is used, end = start. When effectivePeriod has start but no end, end = start.
Full ISO datetime. Null if no period end.
Raw end date from source before any inference. HL7 IG FML maps effectivePeriod.end here. Most other implementations leave null.

sources.comment: HL7 IG FML (normative) explicitly maps effectivePeriod.end to verbatim_end_date — MedicationStatement is the only medication resource where this field is populated

references.notes: verbatim_end_date from effectivePeriod.end

sources.comment: omoponfhir and ETL-German leave verbatim_end_date null for MedicationStatement

references.notes: Effective date handling — verbatim_end_date not set

fields.notes: Default 44787730 (Patient Self-Reported Medication). omoponfhir dynamically adjusts based on basedOn/partOf references. ETL-German uses 32817 (CLAIM). HL7 IG FML derives from category.coding.code.

sources.comment: omoponfhir dynamically adjusts the type concept based on basedOn/partOf references: MedicationRequest=38000177, MedicationAdministration=38000179, MedicationDispense=38000175

references.notes: Dynamic type concept from basedOn/partOf

sources.comment: HL7 IG FML derives drug_type_concept_id from category.coding.code rather than using a static constant

references.notes: drug_type_concept_id from category.coding.code

sources.comment: ETL-German uses 32817 (CLAIM) statically for all medication resources

references.notes: CONCEPT_CLAIM (32817) type concept

fields.notes: Reason for discontinuation. omoponfhir truncates to 20 chars and only populates when status=stopped. HL7 IG FML maps from reason.concept.coding.code.

sources.comment: omoponfhir reads statusReason only when status=stopped, then truncates the text to 20 chars to fit varchar(20)

references.notes: stop_reason from statusReason, truncated to 20 chars

sources.comment: HL7 IG FML maps stop_reason from reason.concept.coding.code — a different FHIR path than statusReason

references.notes: stop_reason from reason.concept.coding.code

fields.notes: Not applicable to MedicationStatement (MedicationRequest-only). Leave null.
Dose amount per administration. ETL-German computes mean of Range when doseRange is used.

sources.comment: ETL-German computes arithmetic mean of doseRange.low and doseRange.high when a Range is used; creates one drug_exposure per dosage entry (fan-out)

references.notes: Quantity from doseQuantity or Range mean

sources.comment: omoponfhir reads doseQuantity.value from the first dosage entry, no Range handling

references.notes: Dosage (quantity, unit, route)

fields.notes: Not directly available. Could be computed from period length / dosing frequency, but no reference implementation does this for MedicationStatement.
Free-text dosage instructions.
SNOMED route code mapped to OMOP Route domain concept. omoponfhir performs full vocabulary lookup. ETL-German uses date-aware concept lookup. This project hardcodes null.

sources.comment: omoponfhir performs a full vocabulary lookup via CodeableConceptUtil.getOmopConceptWithFhirConcept() against the OMOP concept table

references.notes: Dosage quantity, unit, route handling

sources.comment: ETL-German uses date-aware concept lookup (findOmopConcepts.getConcepts()) for SNOMED route codes

references.notes: Route concept lookup with date-aware validity checking

fields.notes: Raw route text. omoponfhir prefers route.text, falls back to coding[0].display.
Vaccine-only field; not applicable to MedicationStatement.
Reporter of the medication statement. omoponfhir filters to Practitioner-typed references only. ETL-German does not map provider for MedicationStatement.

sources.comment: omoponfhir reads informationSource and filters to Practitioner-typed references only before resolving to provider_id

references.notes: Information source to provider, Practitioner filter

sources.comment: ETL-German does not map provider_id for MedicationStatement at all

references.notes: setUpDrugExposure() — no provider_id mapping

fields.notes: Encounter reference. R4 uses 'context'; renamed to 'encounter' in R5.
Not mapped. Null in all reviewed implementations.
Best code by vocabulary priority. omoponfhir uses identifier.value as source value. ETL-German uses ATC code.

sources.comment: omoponfhir uses MedicationStatement.identifier.value (business identifier) as the source value and truncates to 50 chars

references.notes: Medication resolution including source value extraction

sources.comment: ETL-German specifically uses ATC code as the source value for European data

references.notes: 274 lines — ATC code extraction used as drug_source_value

fields.notes: Source vocabulary concept. Placeholder: 0.
Raw dose unit string. omoponfhir prefers .code, falls back to .unit.

references.notes: 46 lines. Only normative mapping for any FHIR medication resource. Covers MedicationStatement only.
Most complete: RxNorm concept lookup, contained reference resolution, route concept lookup, dosage parsing, dynamic type concept from basedOn/partOf, stop_reason truncation to 20 chars.
ATC vocabulary, dose form and route mapping, Range mean quantity, fan-out per dosage entry. Type concept: 32817 (CLAIM).

edge_cases.handling: Skip -- do not create drug_exposure row. Consensus across all implementations.
omoponfhir populates stop_reason from statusReason and still creates the row. This project skips.
This project skips. omoponfhir maps all statuses except entered-in-error.
Start and end date are the same. omoponfhir: setDrugExposureStartDate(date); setDrugExposureEndDate(date).

edge_cases.case: effectivePeriod with start but no end

edge_cases.handling: This project falls back to dateAsserted. omoponfhir sets new Date() (current time) if no date found.
omoponfhir dynamically sets drug_type_concept_id to 38000177 (Prescription written). This project leaves at 44787730.
omoponfhir dynamically sets drug_type_concept_id to 38000179 / 38000175 respectively. This project does not implement.
omoponfhir iterates contained[]. ETL-German pre-indexes via medication_id_map. This project skips (returns null).
This project takes first. ETL-German creates one drug_exposure per dosage entry (fan-out).
This project uses selectBestCoding() by vocabulary priority. omoponfhir uses CodeableConceptUtil.searchConcept().
omoponfhir truncates medicationCodeableConcept.getText() to 50 chars. OMOP CDM field is varchar(50).
omoponfhir truncates to 20. OMOP CDM field is varchar(20).
